title: CleanSpark Q2 FY2024 Financial Results: 163% Revenue Growth
text: CleanSpark reported exceptional second-quarter results for fiscal year 2024, with revenue reaching $111.8 million, representing a 163% year-over-year increase from $42.5 million in the prior year period. The company achieved net income of $126.7 million, a dramatic turnaround from an $18.5 million loss in the same quarter last year. The Bitcoin mining company expanded its hashrate capacity to over 17 EH/s, increasing operational capacity by more than 60% during the quarter. The company extended its geographic footprint by opening new facilities in Mississippi and Georgia. As of March 31, 2024, CleanSpark maintained a robust financial position with: Cash reserves: $323.1 million. Bitcoin holdings: $358.0 million. Total assets: $1.54 billion. Minimal debt at $12.8 million. CEO Zach Bradford stated: These efforts have driven a significant rise in our revenue, underscoring the effectiveness of our strategic initiatives. Adjusted EBITDA surged to $181.8 million compared to $12.7 million in Q2 2023. The company achieved wholesale power costs as low as 1.3 cents per kilowatt hour at wholly-owned sites, with an all-in power cost of 4.3 cents per kilowatt hour.
